jokes.text: I just farted.
jokes.setup: Jeremy greets Leslie enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: He immediately announces he just farted
jokes.explanation: Simple gross-out humor that fits Jeremy's character but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: human equivalent of gas station sushi
jokes.setup: Leslie needs to describe Jeremy Jamm
jokes.punchline: Compares him to something universally acknowledged as terrible
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor that's both specific and universally understood. Perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Jamm and Tammy! 'Jammy.'
jokes.setup: Leslie realizes Jeremy and Tammy are dating
jokes.punchline: Their combined name sounds like a food product
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - sounds like jam but also captures their toxic combination

jokes.text: She saw me nail Gangsta's Paradise, I saw her bite the door guy.
jokes.setup: Explanation of how Jeremy and Tammy met
jokes.punchline: Two equally ridiculous but character-appropriate actions
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - both actions are perfectly in character and equally absurd. Great parallel structure.

jokes.text: Why? Dear God, why?
jokes.setup: Leslie asking when they started dating
jokes.punchline: Her horrified existential response to the pairing
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Leslie's horror at this unholy alliance.

jokes.text: because it's a hotel in Los Angeles, not a wine.
jokes.setup: Joan requested a bottle of Chateau Marmont
jokes.punchline: Leslie explains the obvious confusion
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about celebrity disconnect from reality

jokes.text: No, because he's been famously dead for 60 years.
jokes.setup: Joan asks if they got Buddy Holly to sing
jokes.punchline: Leslie's matter-of-fact response about his death
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with the added punch of 'famously dead' - implies everyone should know this

jokes.text: You said your name was Glenn? Yes, I did.
jokes.setup: April introduces herself as April to Joan
jokes.punchline: Joan mishears/misremembers and April goes along with it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing both Joan's obliviousness and April's deadpan commitment to absurdity

jokes.text: One car, that they all had to fight for.
jokes.setup: Joan gave every audience member a car
jokes.punchline: The dark twist that it was just one car they had to fight over
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the Oprah car giveaway trope. Perfect setup and devastating punchline with great economy.

jokes.text: It means it's a cause for celebration.
jokes.setup: Tom asks what culinary means
jokes.punchline: He gives a completely wrong but confident definition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - confident ignorance followed by immediate action based on that ignorance

jokes.text: In my experience with butt faces, you are one.
jokes.setup: Ron makes a sophisticated economic argument
jokes.punchline: Leslie responds with a childish insult using his own structure
jokes.explanation: Excellent structural comedy - mirrors Ron's formal language but with juvenile content. Perfect Leslie regression.

jokes.text: She thinks she's in Batman.
jokes.setup: Joan calling people 'Commissioner Gordon, people of Gotham'
jokes.punchline: Someone pointing out her obvious delusion
jokes.explanation: Simple observational joke about Joan's disconnection from reality.

jokes.text: Please don't say Hitler. Adolf Hitler!
jokes.setup: Leslie preemptively asks Jeremy not to mention Hitler
jokes.punchline: He immediately does exactly that
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-punchline structure. The inevitability makes it funnier, not less.

jokes.text: It's a stache thing. Stache bros.
jokes.setup: Jeremy sides with Ron against Leslie
jokes.punchline: His reasoning is pure mustache solidarity
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully stupid reasoning that's perfectly in character for Jeremy. 'Stache bros' is inherently funny.

jokes.text: On a separate note, I think that America should have a purge night.
jokes.setup: Joan gives inspirational advice about following your passion
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivots to advocating for legalized murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect tonal whiplash from inspirational to horrifying. The casual transition makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That's my cousin. That's my dad!
jokes.setup: Andy suggesting women for Tom to date from his social media
jokes.punchline: Both suggestions are inappropriate family members
jokes.explanation: Straightforward escalation joke but gets the job done efficiently

jokes.text: Why are you only friends with your family?
jokes.setup: Tom's social media only shows family members
jokes.punchline: Andy's innocent question highlights Tom's social isolation
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that reveals character depth through Andy's oblivious honesty

jokes.text: No, you got all sneaky and snuck around and snooked that vote away from me.
jokes.setup: Leslie is angry at Ron for getting the vote
jokes.punchline: Her increasing use of 'snook' variations
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay that shows Leslie's tendency to over-elaborate when angry. 'Snooked' is particularly funny.

jokes.text: The snooker has become the snorked!
jokes.setup: Continuation of Leslie's snook variations
jokes.punchline: She creates an even more absurd variant
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the wordplay bit. 'Snorked' sounds completely made up but confident.

jokes.text: Are you horny with gratitude?
jokes.setup: Tammy asking if her vote manipulation worked on Ron
jokes.punchline: Her hilariously direct and inappropriate phrasing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tammy line - direct, sexual, and completely inappropriate. The phrase 'horny with gratitude' is inherently funny.

jokes.text: Are you horny with gratitude?
jokes.setup: Tammy asking if her vote switching worked to win Ron back
jokes.punchline: Her characteristically inappropriate phrasing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tammy line - sexual and inappropriate in exactly her characteristic way.

jokes.text: It's like rescuing a bunny from a lion. But that lion is a demonic sociopath with really nice cleavage.
jokes.setup: Describing the difficulty of separating Jamm from Tammy
jokes.punchline: Escalating the metaphor with specific Tammy details
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor that escalates nicely and captures both characters well.

jokes.text: Chicago! The big apple.
jokes.setup: Andy and Tom arrive in Chicago
jokes.punchline: Andy calls it by New York's nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - confident ignorance about basic geography

jokes.text: That's what murderers do before they murder someone.
jokes.setup: Andy wants to show up unannounced at Lucy's workplace
jokes.punchline: Tom's extreme interpretation of this plan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that takes a reasonable concern to an extreme conclusion

jokes.text: You're not gonna murder me, are you?
jokes.setup: Tom just explained this behavior is what murderers do
jokes.punchline: Lucy immediately asks the obvious question
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback that acknowledges the absurdity of the situation

jokes.text: Head coach of the Chicago Bulls. No, he's working at a non-profit. No. What? Am I?
jokes.setup: Andy needs to explain what job he's getting in Chicago
jokes.punchline: His completely unrealistic suggestion and genuine confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy comedy - absurd confidence followed by genuine confusion about his own life

jokes.text: I'm just gonna go live under a bridge and ask people riddles before they cross.
jokes.setup: April doesn't know what career path to take
jokes.punchline: Her completely absurd but oddly specific alternative plan
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird April line that's both specific and completely in character. The fairy tale reference makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: I'm just gonna go live under a bridge and ask people riddles before they cross.
jokes.setup: April saying she has to quit her job
jokes.punchline: Her characteristically dark alternative career plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response - dark, literary reference, completely impractical but said with conviction.

jokes.text: That it doesn't count as stripping if no one pays you?
jokes.setup: Ben asks if April remembers what Joan said
jokes.punchline: April recalls the wrong (and inappropriate) quote
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - she remembers the most inappropriate thing instead of the inspirational message

jokes.text: People are dying to get in.
jokes.setup: Dan welcomes them to the funeral home
jokes.punchline: Classic funeral home pun
jokes.explanation: Standard funeral home pun - predictable but appropriate for the character and setting

jokes.text: People are dying to get in.
jokes.setup: Mortician welcoming visitors
jokes.punchline: Classic death-related pun
jokes.explanation: Extremely predictable mortician pun. Functional but hacky.

jokes.text: She has my stool analyzed just to keep me honest.
jokes.setup: Jeremy explains Tammy's dietary control
jokes.punchline: The disturbing extent of her monitoring
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disgusting detail that shows how far Tammy's control extends. Gross but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: And you have a Hooters platinum card.
jokes.setup: Listing things that make Jeremy who he is
jokes.punchline: The existence of such a ridiculous status symbol
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that's both specific to Jamm and absurdly plausible.

jokes.text: But it just feels right.
jokes.setup: Jeremy lists all the ways he's miserable with Tammy
jokes.punchline: He concludes this terrible situation 'feels right'
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - he describes hell and concludes it's good. Shows how broken he is.

jokes.text: More hair came out.
jokes.setup: Jeremy having a breakdown about his relationship
jokes.punchline: Physical manifestation of his stress getting worse
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing the literal toll Tammy is taking on him.

jokes.text: Dammit, Ron! Engage in the culture once in a while!
jokes.setup: Ron hasn't seen Monsters, Inc.
jokes.punchline: Leslie's frustrated outburst about his cultural isolation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's frustration with Ron's deliberate cultural ignorance

jokes.text: You keep that in your desk?
jokes.setup: Ron producing a gold bar
jokes.punchline: Jeremy focusing on the wrong unusual aspect
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Jeremy missing the bigger point about being offered gold.

jokes.text: Tammy does not abide by the Geneva Convention.
jokes.setup: Ron explains the seriousness of fighting Tammy
jokes.punchline: He treats her like a war criminal
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that treats relationship drama like international warfare. Perfect Ron delivery.

jokes.text: She lets me rub up against her leg sometimes.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jeremy and Tammy's sex life
jokes.punchline: His pathetically limited sexual contact
jokes.explanation: Appropriately pathetic and disturbing detail that shows Jeremy's complete subjugation

jokes.text: Why is every job just paperwork?
jokes.setup: Learning about mortician requirements
jokes.punchline: April's existential complaint about all careers
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that resonates with anyone who's looked into career changes.

jokes.text: being embalmed by some weirdo who had to go to school for three years just to cut my guts open.
jokes.setup: April realizes all jobs involve paperwork
jokes.punchline: Her morbid but accurate description of mortician training
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - takes the situation to its logical dark conclusion in her characteristically blunt way

jokes.text: Hey there, horsey. Time to mount up and ride on into Bonertown.
jokes.setup: Leslie role-playing as Tammy to train Jeremy
jokes.punchline: Her hilariously inappropriate Tammy impression
jokes.explanation: Funny because it's Leslie trying to be Tammy - completely inappropriate but necessary for the training

jokes.text: She survived on rats and rainwater.
jokes.setup: Ron explains Tammy camping in his attic
jokes.punchline: The disturbing details of her survival
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that shows Tammy's terrifying dedication. Specific details make it funnier.

jokes.text: Hey, this is why they call it Beantown, huh?
jokes.setup: Tom is in Chicago and deflecting from awkwardness
jokes.punchline: He uses Boston's nickname for Chicago
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment - trying to seem knowledgeable but getting it wrong

jokes.text: Come work for me in Pawnee?
jokes.setup: Tom seemingly about to ask Lucy on a date
jokes.punchline: Instead offering her a job
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that subverts romantic expectations with business proposition.

jokes.text: Literally. All the steak and whiskey, I have to wear a diaper.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the relationship is unhealthy
jokes.punchline: Revealing the gross physical consequence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the literal physical toll of Tammy's control.

jokes.text: That's 'cause you're my widdle baby!
jokes.setup: Jeremy mentioning wearing diapers
jokes.punchline: Tammy's twisted baby talk response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tammy response - turning something humiliating into twisted affection.

jokes.text: Look at my boobs. No. Look downstairs. No. There's a prize inside for you.
jokes.setup: Tammy trying to seduce Jeremy back
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly desperate and inappropriate offers
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Tammy's desperation and Jeremy's newfound resistance

jokes.text: Turns out the crotch blinder was inside you all along.
jokes.setup: Jeremy successfully resists Tammy without the device
jokes.punchline: Ron's wise observation referencing the earlier prop
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that works as both comedy and character growth moment. Good payoff structure.

jokes.text: I got banned from every riverboat in Germany.
jokes.setup: Donna explains Saturn's Return effects
jokes.punchline: Her specific and mysterious past incident
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna line - mysterious, specific, and completely unexplained. 'Every riverboat in Germany' is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: No, I'm not. I've never cared for you.
jokes.setup: Ben says April is his friend
jokes.punchline: April's immediate and blunt denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response - completely honest and deflating in the moment

jokes.text: No, I'm not. I've never cared for you.
jokes.setup: Ben saying April is his friend
jokes.punchline: April's characteristic denial of caring
jokes.explanation: Classic April defensive response to emotional vulnerability.

jokes.text: Although, if I had to pick one side, it would be Lucy Liu's backside.
jokes.setup: Jeremy explaining he can't choose between Leslie and Ron
jokes.punchline: His completely unrelated and inappropriate comment
jokes.explanation: Typical Jeremy non-sequitur that's inappropriate but in character

jokes.text: Five-second rule!
jokes.setup: Andy finds abandoned spaghetti in the restaurant
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic application of the five-second rule
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Andy moment - gross but perfectly in character
